title: The Way.
content:
When I a ship see on the seas,
    Cuff'd with those wat'ry savages,
    And therewithal behold it hath
    In all that way no beaten path,
    Then, with a wonder, I confess
    Thou art our way i' th' wilderness;
    And while we blunder in the dark,
    Thou art our candle there, or spark.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "The Way.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
